heading: Summer fill and fuel budgeting programs
label: 6283 Summer fill and fuel budgeting programs
notes: Amendments2005—Subsec. (e). struck out heading and text of subsec. (e). Text read as follows: “ does not apply to this section.”Pub. L. 109–58Section 6285 of this title
amendments: Amendments2005—Subsec. (e). struck out heading and text of subsec. (e). Text read as follows: “ does not apply to this section.”Pub. L. 109–58Section 6285 of this title

children.heading: Budget contract
children.label: 1 Budget contract
children.text: The term “budget contract” means a contract between a retailer and a consumer under which the heating expenses of the consumer are spread evenly over a period of months.

children.heading: Fixed-price contract
children.label: 2 Fixed-price contract
children.text: The term “fixed-price contract” means a contract between a retailer and a consumer under which the retailer charges the consumer a set price for propane, kerosene, or heating oil without regard to market price fluctuations.

children.heading: Price cap contract
children.label: 3 Price cap contract
children.text: The term “price cap contract” means a contract between a retailer and a consumer under which the retailer charges the consumer the market price for propane, kerosene, or heating oil, but the cost of the propane, kerosene, or heating oil may exceed a maximum amount stated in the contract.
to develop education and outreach programs to encourage consumers to fill their storage facilities for propane, kerosene, and heating oil during the summer months; and
to promote the use of budget contracts, price cap contracts, fixed-price contracts, and other advantageous financial arrangements,

children.heading: Preference
children.label: c Preference
children.text: In implementing this section, the Secretary shall give preference to States that contribute public funds or leverage private funds to develop State summer fill and fuel budgeting programs.
such sums as are necessary for each fiscal year thereafter.
